channel.title: R Core on The R Blog
channel.description: Recent content in R Core on The R Blog

item.title: Rousseeuw Prize 2026 Awarded to the R Project
item.description: The Rousseeuw Prize for Statistics is a biennial prize that awards pioneering work in statistical methodology. The prize recognizes a statistical innovation, that is, an outstanding contribution or tool that has had significant impact and found wide application in statistical practice. Five members of the R Core Team have been awarded the Rousseeuw Prize for Statistics for their decades of work building and maintaining the R Project. The 2026 laureates are:

item.title: RSMF: Enabling the Next Generation of Contributors to R
item.description: The Software Sustainability Institute (UK) has awarded a grant of £500k (~650k USD) under the Research Software Maintenance Fund, to the project Enabling the Next Generation of Contributors to R. The project is co-led by research software engineers at the University of Birmingham (Heather Turner and Ella Kaye), members of the R Core Team (Simon Urbanek and Peter Dalgaard), and a team of academic/freelance R developers (Gabe Becker, Kylie Bemis, Mikael Jagan, Jeroen Ooms).
